burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant LUTS burden and QOL impact

vulnerablePopulations: men with limited access to urology care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DRE and PSA evaluation, IPSS symptom score, urinalysis; ultrasound and uroflowmetry when indicated

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with access to medications and timely referral

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: SBU-aligned evaluation with DRE, PSA and symptom scoring; TRUS available in reference centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Alpha-blockers and 5-alpha reductase inhibitors via SUS; TURP for refractory cases; limited access to newer minimally invasive options
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Long wait times for elective urology procedures and underdiagnosis in rural areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Standard medications available and TURP capacity in major hospitals
